title: rosa × dumetorum var. hispidula
intro: Rosa × dumetorum var. hispidula, also known as the wild rose, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rosaceae and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum var. hispidula include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever, while also being used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and reduce pain.
